question: A compensation activity inside a loop has completed three times during a process instance. When a compensation throw event in its scope fires, how many times is the associated compensation handler invoked?

options.text: Three times — once for each completion of the compensation activity.
Once, regardless of how many times the activity completed.
Zero, because compensation only runs for terminated activities.
Once per active token in the scope when the throw event fires.

a.text: If an activity has been completed more than once, its compensation handler is invoked the same number of times — three completions, three handler invocations.

b.text: The handler is invoked once per completion of the activity, not just once overall. Three completions yield three invocations.

c.text: Compensation invokes handlers for completed activities, not for terminated ones. Three successful completions are exactly the trigger.

d.text: Invocation count is driven by how many times the activity completed, not by the number of active tokens at the throw event.

explanation: A compensation handler reverts the effects of its associated compensation activity. When the throw event fires, the engine invokes the handler once for each successful completion of the activity — three completions in this case mean three handler invocations.
